title: [security] OSCORE: contradictory SSN rollback docs in oscore.h vs code/test
description:
Reviewed last 20 commits (git log --oneline -20). Relevant changes in 87f90bc7b (oscore.c SECURITY comment update) and ad37f4d3f (oscore.h update, test_nvm_*, Rust lichen-oscore seqnum/protect changes for NVM/SSN handling).

Current state (post-fixes):
- oscore.c:1629-1634 SECURITY comment: MUST rollback SSN on NVM_FAILED to prevent nonce reuse on reboot (conditional rollback under mutex if sender_seq still matches seq+1; sets initialized=true).
- Test in tests/oscore/src/main.c:356-359 expects rollback (ssn remains 100 after failure).
- But oscore.h:488-492 (and 480-486) contains outdated paragraph claiming "SSN increment is NOT rolled back (packet was prepared/transmitted)" and references old line 1604. Contradicts code, test, and other header para at 482-486 which mentions rollback.

This is a P0 security doc bug: incorrect security analysis in public API header for critical nonce-uniqueness property (RFC 8613 §7.2, OSCORE AEAD nonce reuse = catastrophic). Misleads implementers/reviewers about replay/nonce guarantees. Also stale line refs.

No other obvious issues found in MIC, key zeroization, EDHOC, Schnorr, or timing in these commits (Rust uses Zeroizing, replay-before-decrypt, SeqExhausted guards). Rust seqnum::increment now explicitly <= MAX.

No EDHOC/Schnorr changes in last 20.
